title: Completion Contract Loop
summary: Prevents agents from presenting partial progress as done by tracking required outcomes and evidence.
steps: Before acting, turn the goal into a numbered contract of required outcomes and acceptable evidence for each.
After every bounded action, mark each requirement as proved, weak, missing, contradicted, or blocked.
Do not declare completion while any requirement is weak, missing, contradicted, or blocked.
When progress stalls, return the contract table with the next owner and smallest unblock action.
Only complete when every requirement is proved by evidence gathered in the current run or trusted artifacts.
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Completion Contract Loop for [goal, required_outcomes, evidence_policy].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Before acting, turn the goal into a numbered contract of required outcomes and acceptable evidence for each.
- After every bounded action, mark each requirement as proved, weak, missing, contradicted, or blocked.
- Do not declare completion while any requirement is weak, missing, contradicted, or blocked.
- When progress stalls, return the contract table with the next owner and smallest unblock action.
- Only complete when every requirement is proved by evidence gathered in the current run or trusted artifacts.

Quality gates before completion:
- contract exists before work
- requirements map to evidence
- status updated after actions
- completion only when all proved

Stop when one of these is true:
- all requirements proved
- blocked owner needed
- budget exhausted with contract preserved

Finish with:
- requirement-to-evidence table
- action log
- blocked items
- final status

Escalate for human approval before: changing required outcomes, accepting weak evidence as done.
